commands.review_summary:
The PR tightens task owner access to require non-empty normalized task and caller owner keys before owner-scoped lookup succeeds, with regression tests for blank callers and restored blank-owner task records.

PR surface: Source +4, Tests +75. Total +79 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main normalizes blank task and caller owner keys to undefined and then compares them for equality in canOwnerAccessTask. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor proof plus maintainer acceptance of the fail-closed legacy-row behavior; there is no narrow automated code repair indicated by this review.

target.title: Require non-empty owner keys for task access
target.body:
## Summary
- require both task owner keys and caller owner keys to normalize to non-empty values before owner-scoped task access succeeds
- add regression coverage for blank caller keys and restored legacy session tasks with blank owner keys

## Tests
- ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/tasks/task-owner-access.test.ts
- ./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/tasks/task-owner-access.ts src/tasks/task-owner-access.test.ts

Note: initial `pnpm vitest ...` and pre-commit dependency checks attempted `pnpm install`, but sharp's install script failed because node-gyp is not available in this worktree. I completed dependency linking with `pnpm install --ignore-scripts` and ran the scoped binaries directly.
target.author: marksurfas
